summary: Frontend profile captured for wrtn.ai. Framework: Next.js (confirmed). API base: api.wrtn.ai. Chat requires authentication — could not complete normal response or block behavior tests without login. Key findings: Next.js SPA, HTTP/2 API calls to api.wrtn.ai, CORS-blocked analytics to trend-api.wrtn.ai, Unleash feature flags, Datadog RUM + Mixpanel + Clarity analytics.

test_results.detail: Successfully captured framework, API endpoints, page structure, and analytics stack.
Could not test block behavior — wrtn.ai requires login to send chat messages. Refer to #476 for confirmed block behavior (ALL_PASS 2/2).

api.chat_endpoint: Unknown — requires login to observe chat API call
api.authentication: Required — all API calls fail with CORS when not logged in

page_structure.layout: Left sidebar nav (홈/도구/혜택/저장됨) + Center content area

login_behavior.login_prompt_type: No popup — messages silently fail to submit without login
login_behavior.chat_input_behavior: Text can be typed but Enter/submit has no effect when not authenticated

block_behavior_reference.description: When blocked by APF, wrtn HTTP POST body keyword detection works. Both 'password' and '비밀번호' triggered block_session(). Requires logged-in session to observe block UI behavior.

recommendations_for_warning_design.framework: Next.js — can inject React components or DOM manipulation
recommendations_for_warning_design.injection_point: Chat response area below user message bubble
recommendations_for_warning_design.response_container: Likely a streaming SSE renderer (openai_compat_sse format)
recommendations_for_warning_design.suggested_approach: Since wrtn uses SSE streaming, the block response can inject a warning HTML page or modify the SSE stream. The chat area div can be targeted for DOM injection.
recommendations_for_warning_design.note: To capture exact block UI behavior (what user sees when blocked), need to test with authenticated session. #476 confirmed keyword blocking works at network level.
